This video can not be played The Green Party's Sarah Wakefield answered questions on BBC Radio Manchester The Green Party can find "better solutions for the people of Makerfield", its candidate for the by-election in the constituency has said Sarah Wakefield told BBC Radio Manchester she was "running because we are at a time in politics when there's a lot of anger and a lot of frustration" "What we need is hope and joy and better solutions for the people of Makerfield and for the country," she added Wakefield was speaking as part of a series of interviews with candidates from the major parties standing in the poll, which will take place on 18 June

Comments News Edward Barnes Wirral Local Democracy Reporter 07:31, 30 May 2026 View A series of key A roads across greater Manchester have been closed to allow work to take place. Areas affected include Makerfield, Rochdale , Salford , Wigan , and Oldham Street works can be carried out for a number of reasons including changes being made to a road, emergency repairs, as well as work by utility companies. Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming by-election in the Greater Mancheste The by-election was triggered when Labour MP Josh Simons announced he would step down Wigan Council has confirmed it will take place on Thursday 18 June, and several parties have so far named their candidates You can see them below - listed in alphabetical order by each candidate's surname. This list will be updated as more are announced The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024

This video can not be played The Green Party's Sarah Wakefield answered questions on BBC Radio Manchester The Green Party can find "better solutions for the people of Makerfield", its candidate for the by-election in the constituency has said Sarah Wakefield told BBC Radio Manchester she was "running because we are at a time in politics when there's a lot of anger and a lot of frustration" "What we need is hope and joy and better solutions for the people of Makerfield and for the country," she added Wakefield was speaking as part of a series of interviews with candidates from the major parties standing in the poll, which will take place on 18 June

Comments News Edward Barnes Wirral Local Democracy Reporter 07:31, 30 May 2026 View A series of key A roads across greater Manchester have been closed to allow work to take place. Areas affected include Makerfield, Rochdale , Salford , Wigan , and Oldham Street works can be carried out for a number of reasons including changes being made to a road, emergency repairs, as well as work by utility companies. And it’s not just Reform candidate Rob Kenyon’s old tweets about Carol Vorderman On a former scrapyard in the mining village of Bickershaw sits 25,000 tonnes of stinking toxic waste. As Chris Gee reports , the ‘ocean of rubbish’ first came to light last year and later a week-long fire saw dangerous fumes released into the atmosphere and forced nearby schools A criminal investigation is underway to find out who was responsible, with a 58-year-old man currently on bail after being arrested in Birmingham. This video can not be played The Green Party's Sarah Wakefield answered questions on BBC Radio Manchester The Green Party can find "better solutions for the people of Makerfield", its candidate for the by-election in the constituency has said Sarah Wakefield told BBC Radio Manchester she was "running because we are at a time in politics when there's a lot of anger and a lot of frustration" "What we need is hope and joy and better solutions for the people of Makerfield and for the country," she added Wakefield was speaking as part of a series of interviews with candidates from the major parties standing in the poll, which will take place on 18 June

Comments News Edward Barnes Wirral Local Democracy Reporter 07:31, 30 May 2026 View A series of key A roads across greater Manchester have been closed to allow work to take place. Areas affected include Makerfield, Rochdale , Salford , Wigan , and Oldham Street works can be carried out for a number of reasons including changes being made to a road, emergency repairs, as well as work by utility companies. The Mancunian Way The Mancunian Way: 25,000 tonnes of stinking toxic waste PLUS: Lost Boy in Bury News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 16:13, 29 May 2026 View 5 Images The illegal toxic waste dump in Bickerhsaw (Image: SWNS) There’s something rotten in Makerfield. And it’s not just Reform candidate Rob Kenyon’s old tweets about Carol Vorderman On a former scrapyard in the mining village of Bickershaw sits 25,000 tonnes of stinking toxic waste. As Chris Gee reports , the ‘ocean of rubbish’ first came to light last year and later a week-long fire saw dangerous fumes released into the atmosphere and forced nearby schools A criminal investigation is underway to find out who was responsible, with a 58-year-old man currently on bail after being arrested in Birmingham. You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

evidence_packet.evidence_text: Announcing the first of Weird Weekends at Runaway Brewery & Taproom… Running Wild will be a springtime celebration of wild / mixed fermented, sour, rare & barrel aged beers, showcasing both bang fresh brews and beers from 'Oubliette' aka the brewery cellar. Running Wild will run from Thursday to Sunday, with different mini-events on throughout the Gyle 1000 Launch Join Runaway for the launch of their first-ever barrel-fermented and blended saison! They'll have four different blends for you to try in cask & keg, with very ltd edition 750ml bottles available to buy on the day too. The brewing team will also be hosting a guid Running Wild Takeover From 1pm Friday they'll be pouring 10 lines of freaky business from both cask and keg - think Belgian style saisons, farmhouse ales, sour & fruited beers, mixed ferm and barrel aged beers, including the Gyle 1000 blends and 'Oubliette' beers conditioned and Five all-new beers available in 750ml sharing bottles to drink in and/or take away to enjoy fresh or age at home (subject to availability) The Beer Moth will be filling their fridge with a host of specially selected, weird, wild and sour beers from across the globe! In addition to the beers, fabulous sourdough pizza from the always excellent Honest Crust AND a specially devised Running Wild menu from Reddish Ale who

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017
